briefdescription.para: PCIe OB window within any address range.
PCIe OB window within 32-bit address range.
PCIe OB window above 32-bit address range.
Callback API for PCIe reset interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: These callbacks execute in interrupt context. Therefore, use only interrupt-safe APIS. Registration of callbacks is done via pcie_ep_register_reset_cb

parameterdescription.para: Pointer provided at registration time, later to be passed back as argument to callback function

detaileddescription.para: This API reads EP's own configuration space

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to data read from the offset

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t offset, uint32_t data)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_conf_write

detaileddescription.para: This API writes EP's own configuration space

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Data to be written at the offset

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint64_t pcie_addr, uint64_t *mapped_addr, uint32_t size, enum pcie_ob_mem_type ob_mem_type)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_map_addr

briefdescription.para: Map a host memory buffer to PCIe outbound region.

detaileddescription.para: This API maps a host memory buffer to PCIe outbound region, It is left to EP driver to manage multiple mappings through multiple PCIe outbound regions if supported by SoC

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Host memory buffer address to be mapped
Hint if lowmem/highmem outbound region has to be used, this is useful in cases where bus master cannot generate more than 32-bit address; it becomes essential to use lowmem outbound region

simplesect.para: Mapped size : If mapped size is less than requested size, then requester has to call the same API again to map the unmapped host buffer after data transfer is done with mapped size. This situation may arise because of the mapping alignment requirements.

briefdescription.para: Remove mapping to PCIe outbound region.

detaileddescription.para: This API removes mapping to PCIe outbound region. Mapped PCIe outbound region address is given as argument to figure out the outbound region to be unmapped

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum pci_ep_irq_type irq_type, uint32_t irq_num)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_raise_irq

detaileddescription.para: This API raises interrupt to Host

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Type of Interrupt be raised (legacy, MSI or MSI-X)

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum pcie_reset reset, pcie_ep_reset_callback_t cb, void *arg)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_register_reset_cb

briefdescription.para: Register callback function for reset interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: If reset interrupts are handled by device, this API can be used to register callback function, which will be executed part of corresponding PCIe reset handler

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Argument to be passed back to callback function

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint64_t mapped_addr, uintptr_t local_addr, uint32_t size, const enum xfer_direction dir)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_dma_xfer

briefdescription.para: Data transfer between mapped Host memory and device memory with "System DMA".

detaileddescription.para: The term "System DMA" is used to clarify that we are not talking about dedicated "PCIe DMA"; rather the one which does not understand PCIe address directly, and uses the mapped Host memory.
If DMA controller is available in the EP device, this API can be used to achieve data transfer between mapped Host memory, i.e. outbound memory and EP device's local memory with DMA

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint64_t pcie_addr, uintptr_t *local_addr, uint32_t size, enum pcie_ob_mem_type ob_mem_type, enum xfer_direction dir)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_xfer_data_memcpy

detaileddescription.para: Helper API to achieve data transfer with memcpy through PCIe outbound memory

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Hint if lowmem/highmem outbound region has to be used (PCIE_OB_LOWMEM / PCIE_OB_HIGHMEM / PCIE_OB_ANYMEM), should be PCIE_OB_LOWMEM if bus master cannot generate more than 32-bit address

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint64_t pcie_addr, uintptr_t *local_addr, uint32_t size, enum pcie_ob_mem_type ob_mem_type, enum xfer_direction dir)
memberdef.name: pcie_ep_xfer_data_dma

detaileddescription.para: Helper API to achieve data transfer with system DMA through PCIe outbound memory, this API is based off pcie_ep_xfer_data_memcpy, here we use "system dma" instead of memcpy

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Hint if lowmem/highmem outbound region has to be used (PCIE_OB_LOWMEM / PCIE_OB_HIGHMEM / PCIE_OB_ANYMEM)

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Public APIs for the PCIe EP drivers.
